assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: God's Big Picture

➜ INTRO
You will be reading God's Big Picture slowly over the entire year. Take a minute to acquaint yourself with the structure of the book. Notice the charts included in the text; don't skip these as you read the assigned selections—they are important supports. You may wish to read through the Bible study section at the end of each chapter but they are not generally assigned.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
God's Big Picture p.9-16
Preface and Introduction up to middle of p.16

⍞ Materials: God's Big Picture

➜ READ
p.16-23, continuing Introduction

➜ WRITE
Reflect on Fig.5 (p.22); consider how this applied to your Old Testament readings last year. Keep it in mind as you read this year's selections, perhaps using it as the basis for some of your Bible journal writings during other Bible lessons.

➜ READ
Use any extra lesson time today to finish other Bible lessons or do some reading from books suggested in Extra Helpings.

➜ READ
p.23-26, continuing Introduction

assignment_teachersNote:
★ Bible History: Grade 9 — TERM 1 EXAM QUESTIONS
· God's Big Picture: Choose one: 
a) What does the author mean by “God's people, God's place, and God's rule and blessing”?
b) List the Old Testament covenants and explain how God's grace is shown through them AND Tell what you know about the "new covenant"
c) Create The Kingdom of God Chart for “The pattern of the kingdom, The perished kingdom, and The promised kingdom.”

⍞ Materials: God's Big Picture, "Four Views of Revelation"

➜ INTRO
Chapter 8, "The Perfected Kingdom", covers topics from Revelation. Theologians have interpreted the timeline of events differently throughout church history. The four main views are mentioned briefly in this text. An article by Dr. Patrick Zukeran is provided for a bit more context. Please note that though this is a fairly balanced overview, he does tend toward the futurist view.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.147-150

➜ READ & DISCUSS
∞ Article Link: "Four Views of Revelation"
For a bit more information, read Zukeran's article.
